summary: High‑energy spring ’85 audience tape with crisp sound, standout Supplication jam and vibrant crowd moments
review: The recording captures the electric vibe of a spring‑1985 show – audiences singing “Rain” and “Women Are Smarter,” a sweet Supplication jam, Tennessee Jed and Brent’s bluesy “Baby.”  While it’s an audience tape, reviewers praise its surprisingly clear mix and lack of low‑end distortion, making it one of the better ’85 auds on the Archive.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4–5 stars and highlight strong performance energy plus good‑to‑excellent sound quality; only one moderate rating and a non‑relevant low star comment, so the combined show (70 %) and recording (30 %) score is high.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners note clear mids, less low‑end distortion than comparable board recordings, a sweet overall balance and occasional “nice sound” remarks; no major drop‑outs reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set rides a high‑energy wave – crowd‑driven sing‑alongs on Women Are Smarter and Cold Rain and Snow, a memorable Supplication jam leading into a xylophone‑pre‑drum segment, then bluesy Brent showcase; overall pacing is praised as lively and engaging.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Fans recall a girl dancing in front of Jerry during Women Are Smarter, noting his amused reaction (“JER BEAR”).
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s witty line about a “Phil loves New York” bumper‑sticker is quoted, adding to the show’s fun vibe.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s “Baby” solo receives specific praise; reviewers also comment on the clean sound possibly from a Schoeps rig.
band_member_comments.Drums: A pre‑drum jam featuring xylophones is highlighted as an exciting moment.
